author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg:
The team never set a device limit, but too many phones on one ISP leads to duplicate tasks, which is worthless to the network. A fixed limit wouldn't work anyway—people would just hit the cap without improving coverage.

We need geographic spread across many ISPs and countries. That’s the only product that attracts Telecom partners and real work. Over-concentration makes the data useless, and the team will be forced to cut devices in those areas. What happens to the farms then?

author: SpideyNode616
msg:
question, and this is more just opinion based....

1. is it safe to assume the $2 monthly fee doesnt start until Unity is equally availible on both iOS and Android?

2. At what kind of income per month is it considered acceptable to add the $2 fee per month? 

Even at $5 per month, pre split. thats $2.50 each...... if the ULO is going to pay for that theselves, at that point 50c a month profit. not worth it.....

Just curious how much we would personally expect/like for monthly revenue PRE split for it to be worth introducing the credit fee?

I have my opinion...... I think $1 per day, $30 per month..... so with a $15 each on a 50/50 split, whoever pays the credit fee is still making good money.

I was thinking about it today, just curious others thoughts on what they would expect

author: Wen (calibrating)
msg:
Nobody is paying $2 per month to earn .50c
Nobody will be $2 per month to earn $5

Need stable regular incentives of at least .5 per day.

author: Rdawgie
msg: I've conducted many test and this is what I have observed so far

author: Rdawgie
msg:
Depending on your setup, I've seen a range of rewards you could fall into.
Range 1: $0.01- This could be for many reasons. Bad uptime,  too many phones on 1 IP, bad router configuration.
Range 2: $0.04 - $0.08 Could also be for the reasons above but not as severe.
Range 3: $0.16 - $0.24 Possibly could tweak router setup. Everything else is ideal
Range 4: $0.26 - $0.40 This is the ideal setup/environment. No tweaking required

author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg:
This was one of the first issues raised by the Telemachus DAO during the second AMA, and we subsequently submitted a report. As a group of more than two members sharing multiple nodes, a multisig wallet would solve several operational problems for us. Josh acknowledged this and replied that the team would look into it. We understand, of course, that there are other current priorities.
And there are no multisig wallets on the world mobile chain that I know off.. yet...

author: JD
msg:
🚨
Guys please do me a favor, for those of you that are still receiving low rewards - try to disable **QoS (Quality of Service)** and all **AI protection settings** in your router (if it's turned ON) and report after 2 days if it made any difference for you. 

I am now finishing my 2 weeks rigorous testing of earning discrepancies with positive results (**I'm now getting full or almost to full rewards where up to this point I was in that low earning group**). 

I will put out full report of my findings here tomorrow or day after tomorrow. But it would be interesting to get your feedback on this specific QoS (and AI protection related settings) setting in the meantime. Thx

msg: All in the announcement

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Development and growth efforts continue across multiple areas simultaneously:

Customer activity on the network has increased, contributing to higher task volume. This growth reflects the continued adoption of the network's infrastructure by commercial partners.

**Work is progressing on several fronts beyond Scout & Runner**, including the expansion of existing task types, the introduction of new tasks, and the onboarding of early third-party partners building on the network.

**We anticipate continued scaling as additional tasks are deployed - both through internal development** and third-party integrations. Official communications will be published as these initiatives progress.

author: Mat
msg:
#amaquestion
As I understand it, the network will support both first-party and third-party tasks. Could the team explain how third-party task code is vetted before deployment? Specifically, I’d like to understand what safeguards are in place to prevent the distributed edge network from being misused by bad actors through deployment of malicious tasks.
